title: CDMGTypes
lean_code_with_comments:
def IsCADMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic

def IsDMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.J = ∅

def IsADMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅

def IsCDG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.L = ∅

def IsDG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅

def IsCDAG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.L = ∅

def IsDAG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅
lean_code_without_comments:
def IsCADMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic

def IsDMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.J = ∅

def IsADMG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅

def IsCDG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.L = ∅

def IsDG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅

def IsCDAG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.L = ∅

def IsDAG (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅
lean_explanation:
The file formalises a vocabulary: it introduces seven `Prop`-valued predicates on `G : CDMG Node` — `IsCADMG`, `IsDMG`, `IsADMG`, `IsCDG`, `IsDG`, `IsCDAG`, `IsDAG` — one per item of the LN definition. Each predicate is the literal conjunction of the LN's stated atomic conditions, written in the order the LN writes them.

The binders are minimal. `Node : Type*` is the ambient vertex universe — the silent carrier $\alpha$ on which the LN's graphs are defined — and the typeclass assumption `[DecidableEq Node]` is inherited from `def_3_1`'s `CDMG` structure, since both the `Finset Node` equality `G.J = ∅` and the `IsAcyclic` predicate of `def_3_6` ultimately decompose into per-element decidable membership tests. A single `variable {Node : Type*} [DecidableEq Node]` line scopes those binders over the whole namespace, so each of the seven predicates takes just a `G : CDMG Node` argument.

Three Mathlib idioms appear. `Finset Node` is Mathlib's type of finite subsets of `Node`, with decidable membership and computable cardinality. The notation `∅` resolves through the `EmptyCollection` typeclass to `Finset.empty` here, so `G.J = ∅` is on-the-nose equality of finite subsets — exactly the LN's $J = \emptyset$. The connective `∧` is right-associative in Lean 4, so the three-conjunct body of `IsDAG` parses as `G.IsAcyclic ∧ (G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅)`; the anonymous-constructor pattern `obtain ⟨hac, hJ, hL⟩ := h` therefore unpacks all three atomic conditions in one destructure.

The shape of each definition is uniform: a single `def IsXxx (G : CDMG Node) : Prop := <conjunction>`, where the right-hand side is drawn from the three atomic conditions `G.IsAcyclic`, `G.J = ∅`, and `G.L = ∅`. Three of the seven (`IsCADMG`, `IsDMG`, `IsCDG`) are single-condition aliases under the LN acronyms; three (`IsADMG`, `IsDG`, `IsCDAG`) are two-conjunct; and `IsDAG` is the strongest, requiring all three. Since the atomic conditions are independent booleans, the seven named predicates together with the bare `CDMG` (no condition) exhaust the $2^3 = 8$ truth-tuples.
design_choices:
**Seven independent `Prop` predicates, not a single `inductive CDMGType` enum.** The LN's "is called" semantics are *nested membership*: a CDMG satisfying the DAG conjunction (item vii) simultaneously bears each of the weaker names (CADMG, DMG, ADMG, CDG, DG, CDAG) and the base CDMG attribute. An `inductive` or sum-type encoding would force exactly one constructor per graph and could not express this overlap; seven independent `Prop`-valued predicates capture it on the nose, so a downstream theorem can carry a single `(h : G.IsDAG)` hypothesis without any tag case-split.

**`def`, not `abbrev`.** The LN treats `CADMG`, `ADMG`, `DAG`, etc., as *named* attributes that reappear under those names in downstream chapters (do-calculus, d-separation, and the iSCM developments all quantify over `IsADMG` or `IsDAG` graphs). An `abbrev` would auto-unfold to the underlying conjunction at every elaboration site, erasing the named abstraction; a plain `def` preserves it. Consumers who want the underlying conjunction can `unfold CDMG.IsDAG` (or `simp only [CDMG.IsDAG]`) on demand.

**`G.J = ∅` and `G.L = ∅` as literal `Finset` equalities, not cardinality or quantifier reformulations.** The LN writes $J = \emptyset$ and $L = \emptyset$ verbatim, and `def_3_1`'s `CDMG.J : Finset Node` and `CDMG.L : Finset (Node × Node)` make these literal equalities of finite subsets. Cardinality (`G.J.card = 0`) or quantifier (`∀ v, v ∉ G.J`) reformulations would force a one-line bridge lemma at every downstream site that case-splits on emptiness, with no semantic gain.

**LN conjunction order preserved.** Item iii reads "acyclic and $J = \emptyset$"; we encode it as `G.IsAcyclic ∧ G.J = ∅`, in that order, not the reverse. Items v, vi, vii likewise preserve LN order. Logically the order is irrelevant, but keeping it aligned means `obtain ⟨hac, hJ⟩ := h` in a downstream proof reads back as "acyclic and `J = ∅`" without re-ordering hypotheses against the LN.

**DG (item v) does *not* require acyclicity.** This is a subtle LN distinction: a "directed graph" without the "acyclic" qualifier is allowed to contain directed cycles, so `IsDG` is `G.J = ∅ ∧ G.L = ∅` only — no `IsAcyclic` conjunct. A reader expecting "DG implies DAG" is wrong; the implication only runs the other direction.

**`IsAcyclic` reused from `def_3_6` verbatim.** Every "$G$ is acyclic" clause is exactly `G.IsAcyclic` — no re-definition, no re-statement. The LN explicitly cross-references the acyclicity definition; mirroring that cross-reference in Lean keeps the dependency graph between chapter-3 rows visible.

**No `Decidable` instances exposed here.** Each predicate is a conjunction of `G.IsAcyclic` (whose decidability is deferred per `def_3_6`'s own design block) and `Finset` equalities (decidable in principle, but only useful when `IsAcyclic` is too). Decidability plumbing is therefore deferred to the use sites — causal-discovery algorithms in later chapters — that actually consume it.
